text: Once during your turn, if you have Solrock in play, you may discard a Basic {F} Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Draw 3 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Lunar Cycle Ability each turn.
If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Accelerating Stab.
If you don’t have Lunatone on your Bench, this attack does nothing. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Search your deck for a Basic {F} Energy card or a Basic {F} Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon {ex}, Pokémon {V}, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
During this turn, attacks used by your {F} Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +100 HP.
Each Stage 2 Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets -30 HP.

Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.
Attach up to 3 Basic {F} Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Mega Brave.
Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.

Discard your hand and draw 5 cards.
Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.
